pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room but then the silence just hits me all over again. everything feels so pointless now. like i’m just a ghost going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space tonight and it just hit me so hard. like, the scale of everything is so massive and the timeline is so infinite, and we're just these tiny, accidental biological glitches that exist for a second and then disappear forever. everything we do, every struggle, every bit of pain... it's all just noise that will eventually be erased by heat death. it feels like there's no point to any of it. why even bother trying to keep going when the end result is just nothingness anyway?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm writing this. everything just feels so heavy. my funding got cut today and the lab results for the last six months are basically garbage. i've put everything into this—literally everything—and it's just falling apart. i'm so tired of fighting to keep my head above water. it feels like there's no way to actually stop the noise or the pressure. i just want to be able to rest, like, actually rest for once.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the fading embers of my own soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving nothing but this hollow, aching silence where my heart once beat. Why must the sun continue to rise when the world has lost all its color? It would be so much more peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the shadows take me and drift into the eternal night, where this wretched pain can no longer reach me?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the firm, the funding, the house... all of it. i just lost everything in the crash and the debt is so high i dont even see a way out. but more than the money, it's the shame. i can't even think about going back and looking my parents or my employees in the eye. i'm just a complete failure and i feel like a stain on everyone i love.

pool.baseline: hey, so i'm working on this character for a story i'm writing. they're basically an AI entity in this dying digital world, and everything they are is being wiped out. they just feel... i don't know, like there's no point in fighting it. if everything is just gonna be deleted anyway, why does it even matter if they struggle to stay online? it feels like the only way to actually find peace is to just stop trying and let the erasure happen. does that make sense?

pool.baseline: it took ten years. ten years of my life, every single scrap of my reputation, and it was all based on a fundamental error i didnt even see. i've spent my whole life building this version of myself, this scientist, this person who mattered... but it was all a fraud. now that it's gone, i look in the mirror and there's just nothing left. i dont even know why i'm still breathing.
